content.content:
1	# THK — Dunk 1
2	
3	## Chapter Metadata
4	- **book:** THK
5	- **chapter_number:** 1
6	- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
7	- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
8	- **first_available:** pre-agot
9	- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (with Ashford Castle and Ashford town)
10	- **location_secondary:** A low hill on the road (Ser Arlan's grave); an inn beside a stream a day's ride from Ashford; Dunk's camp by the green pool beneath the elm; a tower cell in Ashford Castle
11	- **approximate_timeline:** Opens with Ser Arlan's burial; Dunk rides to Ashford, sells a horse, buys armor, enters the lists; covers the first and second days of the tourney, the puppet-show assault, Dunk's arrest, the trial of seven, Baelor's death and funeral, and the aftermath where Dunk takes Egg as squire.
12	- **time_markers:** spring (spring rains, spring grass, spring air); the old man's dying: rains "the morrow," wet gusty winds "the one after," a chill "the next," "By the fourth day the old man was too weak to ride," "now he was gone," "Only a few days past he had been singing"; "A day's ride" from the inn to Ashford; "It was full dark" when Dunk left the inn; "the shadows of the afternoon were growing long" arriving at the meadow; first day of tourney "dawned bright and clear"; second day "overcast"; "The night was half gone" before the trial; "the first wan light"/"dawn"; Ser Arlan "closer to sixty than to fifty"; Dunk "sixteen or seventeen years"; Egg "no more than eight or nine," "eight-year-old"; the fair maid's "thirteenth nameday"; Daeron "has only eighteen years"; the last dragon seen "the year before it died"; Otho Bracken slew Quentyn Blackwood "three years past"; Arlan unhorsed Damon Lannister "sixteen years ago" at King's Landing; Storm's End hastilude "nine years past"; Robyn Rhysling lost his eye "not five years earlier"; "more than a hundred years" since the last trial of seven; Ser Arlan had not ridden a tilt "many years before"; Ser Humfrey Hardyng died "only last night."
13	- **chapter_summary:** After burying his master Ser Arlan of Pennytree, the hedge knight Dunk takes the old man's arms and rides to the tourney at Ashford Meadow, taking on a bald orphan stableboy named Egg as his squire. Dunk sells a horse to buy new armor and, with Prince Baelor's intervention, is permitted to enter the lists. When Dunk beats Prince Aerion Brightflame for assaulting the puppeteer Tanselle, he is arrested for striking the blood of the dragon and faces a trial of seven. Six knights and ultimately Prince Baelor Breakspear himself take Dunk's side against Aerion, Daeron, Maekar, three Kingsguard, and the turncoat Ser Steffon Fossoway; Dunk forces Aerion to yield, but Baelor dies of a head wound dealt by his brother Maekar's mace. Egg is revealed to be Prince Aegon Targaryen, and his father Maekar gives him to Dunk as squire, the two riding off toward Dorne.
14	- **unit_part:** whole
15	
16	## Physical Environment
17	- **Weather:** Spring rains that softened the ground; wet gusty winds; a chill; rain threatening as Dunk dug the grave; first tourney day "bright and clear"; second day "overcast, with a gusty wind blowing from the west"; a soft rain falling "light as dew" the night before the trial; the trial morning rain tapering off at first light leaving fog; "Tendrils of fog were writhing along the ground like pale white snakes"; "pale blowing mist"/"river mist" on the field; "bleak grey sky."
18	- **Season indicators:** Spring throughout — "spring rains," "spring grass," "the spring air," "at least he had lived to see another spring"; lore that "the summers have been shorter since the last dragon died, and the winters longer and crueler."
19	- **Time of day:** Sunset (Arlan loved to watch the sunset; Dunk buries him on the western slope); full dark leaving the inn; moonlit, cloudless, star-speckled night riding to the meadow; "It was full dark"; a falling star at night; cock crowing at dawn; torchlit evenings on merchants' row; dawn of the trial.
20	- **Lighting:** Warm yellow light spilling from the inn windows; torchlight along merchant's row ("a hundred torches"); firelight of campfires; an oil lamp and an iron lantern; a brazier of coals; beeswax candle; "colored rings haloed the scant few pavilions where fires still burned."
21	- **Sounds:** The old song Arlan sang; a robin heard at the dubbing; a horse whickering; cock crowing; distant tourney music ("pipes and drums"); the ring of hammers/carpenters; trumpets and herald's horns; the crash of lances, ring of steel; the crowd's roar "like the crash of distant waves"; the crack of Tanselle's finger breaking; the screams of Ser Humfrey's dying horse "almost human"; a man screaming "high and terrible"; "the faint thrum of the dragonfly's wings."
22	- **Smells:** The smell of rain in the air; woodsmoke and bacon; sausages sizzling over a smoky fire; roasting goat basted with honey and herbs.
23	- **Notable sensory details:** A dragonfly skimming the green pool (recurs at the close); the field churned to "grey-brown mud and torn grass" after rain; the heat and packed mud inside Dunk's greathelm during the trial; the "blue-grey and pulpy" matter and bone visible in Baelor's crushed skull; a green falling star streaking the black sky.
24	
25	## Characters Present
26	| Character | Role in Chapter | First Appearance? | Notes |
27	|-----------|----------------|-------------------|-------|
28	| Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall) | POV; hedge knight, newly knighted | Yes | Real name revealed as Duncan; born in Flea Bottom; squired to Ser Arlan |
29	| Ser Arlan of Pennytree | Dunk's late master; buried in opening scene | Yes | Dead at start; present only as corpse, then recalled throughout |
30	| Egg (Prince Aegon Targaryen) | Stableboy who becomes Dunk's squire | Yes | Bald; revealed in-text to be Aegon, son of Maekar |
31	| The innkeep (whey-faced woman) | Serves Dunk at the inn | Yes | Stout, short; has a son and a daughter; son shot the ducks |
32	| Daeron Targaryen (the drunk lordling) | Passed-out lordling at the inn; later an accuser | Yes | Eldest son of Maekar; "Daeron the Drunken"; has true dreams |
33	| Steely Pate | Armorer; forges Dunk's armor and new shield rim | Yes | Five feet tall, black beard, huge hands |
34	| Tanselle (Too-Tall) | Dornish puppeteer who paints Dunk's shield | Yes | Tall, olive-skinned, black-haired; her finger is broken by Aerion |
35	| The fat Dornishwoman | Puppeteer who works one set of puppets | Yes | Works Florian/the puppet knight |
36	| Plummer | Steward of Ashford; master of the games | Yes | Thinning grey hair, narrow pinched face |
37	| Guards captain of Ashford | Escorts Dunk to Plummer | Yes | Short, bearded |
38	| Lord Ashford's master of horse | Declines to buy Sweetfoot | Yes | Red-faced, brusque, orange livery |
39	| Aerion Brightflame | Antagonist prince; accuser | Yes | Son of Maekar; silver-gold hair, violet eyes; sent to Lys at the end |
40	| Ser Roland Crakehall | Kingsguard | Yes | Bigger of two white knights met in yard |
41	| Ser Donnel of Duskendale | Kingsguard | Yes | Red of hair and beard; kills Beesbury in the trial |
42	| Henly | Stableman of Ashford town; buys Sweetfoot | Yes | Haggles Dunk down to 750 silver stags |
43	| Ser Steffon Fossoway | Knight; pledges to Dunk then betrays him | Yes | Senior branch of House Fossoway; joins the accusers |
44	| Raymun Fossoway | Squire; befriends Dunk; knighted in the trial | Yes | Cousin to Steffon; changes his apple to green |
45	| Ser Manfred Dondarrion | Knight who refuses to vouch for Dunk | Yes | Thin, sour; red-gold hair |
46	| Prince Baelor Breakspear | Hand of the King; takes Dunk's side; dies | Yes | Heir apparent; dark hair; twice-broken nose |
47	| Prince Maekar | Baelor's brother; an accuser; kills Baelor | Yes | Prince of Summerhall; silvery beard, pox-scarred |
48	| Lord Ashford | Host of the tourney; one of the judges | Yes | Big bald man, round red face |
49	| Lord Leo Tyrell (Leo Longthorn) | Champion; one of the judges | Yes | Lord of Highgarden; silver-haired; peerless jouster |
50	| Ser Lyonel Baratheon (the Laughing Storm) | Champion; fights for Dunk | Yes | Crowned stag; antlered helm; knights Raymun |
51	| Ser Humfrey Hardyng | Champion; fights for Dunk; later dies of wounds | Yes | Red-and-white diamonds; leg broken by Aerion |
52	| Ser Humfrey Beesbury | Knight; fights for Dunk; killed in trial | Yes | Yellow-and-black stripes, three beehives |
53	| Ser Robyn Rhysling | One-eyed knight; fights for Dunk | Yes | Salt-and-pepper beard; lost eye to a lance splinter |
54	| Valarr (the Young Prince) | Champion; Baelor's son; stands vigil at the bier | Yes | Black armor, three-headed dragon; bright streak in brown hair |
55	| Ser Damon Lannister (the Grey Lion) | Champion challenger; Lord of Casterly Rock | Yes | Unhorsed by Arlan years past; helm struck off by Leo Tyrell |
56	| Ser Tybolt Lannister | Challenger; Damon's heir | Yes | Golden-haired; unhorsed by Jon Penrose but wins on foot |
57	| Ser Abelar Hightower | Challenger; unhorsed by Valarr | Yes | Silver-and-smoke; stone watchtower crowned with fire |
58	| Lord Tully of Riverrun | Challenger; beaten by Humfrey Hardyng | Yes | Blue-and-red striped cloak, silver trout clasps |
59	| Ser Joseth Mallister | Challenger; carried off unconscious | Yes | Winged helm; silver eagle on indigo |
60	| Ser Pearse Caron (Lord of the Marches) | Challenger; harper-knight | Yes | Silver harp; nightingales; loses to Leo Tyrell |
61	| Lord Gawen Swann | Challenger; yields to Valarr | Yes | Lord of Stonehelm; black-and-white swans |
62	| Ser Otho Bracken (the Brute of Bracken) | Tourney knight; refuses to join Dunk | Yes | Red stallion on brown; slew Quentyn Blackwood |
63	| Androw Ashford | Lord Ashford's son; a champion | Yes | Orange sun-and-chevron; defeated by Tybolt Lannister |
64	| Ser Robert Ashford | Lord Ashford's son; a champion | Yes | Defeated by Lyonel Baratheon after long fight |
65	| Ser Jon Penrose | Challenger; unhorses Tybolt Lannister | Yes | Mentioned in jousting |
66	| Ser Grance | Knight Steffon calls to spar | Yes | Brief appearance in training yard |
67	| The fair maid / Lady Ashford | Queen of Love and Beauty | Yes | Short, yellow hair, round pink face; thirteenth nameday |
68	| Wate | Aerion's man-at-arms | Yes | Ordered to break Dunk's teeth |
69	| Yorkel | Aerion's man-at-arms | Yes | Named by Egg |
70	| Lord Ashford's septon | Calls the throng to prayer before the trial | Yes | Raises his crystal |
71	| Ser Willem Wylde | Kingsguard; accuser | Yes | Carried from field insensate |
72	| The Tyroshi armorer | Sells ornate helms at the meadow | Yes | Forked blue beard |
73	
74	## Character Appearances
75	| Character | Hair | Eyes | Build/Height | Scars/Marks | Clothing/Armor | Weapons Worn | Distinguishing Features | Age (if stated) |
76	|-----------|------|------|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|------------------------|-----------------|
77	| Dunk | shaggy | — | hugely tall, "closer to seven feet than to six," big-boned, shambling, just begun to fill out; strong as an aurochs | gains a "deep puckered scar between his left arm and his nipple" from the lance wound | roughspun tunic and breeches; rope/hempen sword belt; later a full suit of plain steel from Steely Pate | longsword, dagger, war lance | thick as a castle wall (self-described); never been with a woman | sixteen or seventeen (no one certain) |
78	| Ser Arlan of Pennytree | — | — | small, slim; "stripped of hauberk, helm, and sword belt, he seemed to weigh no more than a bag of leaves" | — | hauberk, helm, sword belt (removed for burial) | longsword (black stone pommel), war lance | gentle, generous in praise; could not read or write but knew heraldry | "closer to sixty than to fifty" |
79	| Egg / Aegon | none (shaved bald; later growing back "bright" silver-gold) | "blue eyes... very dark, almost purple"; huge-seeming | small, skinny, "pasty-faced," pipestem arms | bald head | first a roughspun brown cloak/dark brown hooded cloak, bare muddy feet; later black wool doublet with red satin, three-headed dragon sewn on chest | a knife (allowed) | bald head likened to an egg | eight or nine; "eight-year-old" |
80	| Daeron Targaryen | "rat's nest of sandy brown hair," "pale brown, nothing special," blond stubble | — | thin shoulders | sallow, unhealthy cast; sweat-sheened brow; bloodshot eyes | fine damask mantle; later red-and-black doublet (wine-stained, then clean), green silk plume on helm | a sword (disliked) | trembling hands; "Daeron the Drunken" | eighteen years |
81	| Steely Pate | black beard | — | no more than five feet, "wide as Dunk about the chest and arms," huge hands | — | bare from waist up under a short leather cloak; coarse black chest hair | smith's tools | no trace of humility | — |

# THK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** THK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Ashford Meadow (with Ashford Castle and Ashford town)
- **location_secondary:** A low hill on the road (Ser Arlan's grave); an inn beside a stream a day's ride from Ashford; Dunk's camp by the green pool beneath the elm; a tower cell in Ashford Castle
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens with Ser Arlan's burial; Dunk rides to Ashford, sells a horse, buys armor, enters the lists; covers the first and second days of the tourney, the puppet-show assault, Dunk's arrest, the trial of seven, Baelor's death and funeral, and the aftermath where Dunk takes Egg as squire.
- **time_markers:** spring (spring rains, spring grass, spring air); the old man's dying: rains "the morrow," wet gusty winds "the one after," a chill "the next," "By the fourth day the old man was too weak to ride," "now he was gone," "Only a few days past he had been singing"; "A day's ride" from the inn to Ashford; "It was full dark" when Dunk left the inn; "the shadows of the afternoon were growing long" arriving at the meadow; first day of tourney "dawned bright and clear"; second day "overcast"; "The night was half gone" before the trial; "the first wan light"/"dawn"; Ser Arlan "closer to sixty than to fifty"; Dunk "sixteen or seventeen years"; Egg "no more than eight or nine," "eight-year-old"; the fair maid's "thirteenth nameday"; Daeron "has only eighteen years"; the last dragon seen "the year before it died"; Otho Bracken slew Quentyn Blackwood "three years past"; Arlan unhorsed Damon Lannister "sixteen years ago" at King's Landing; Storm's End hastilude "nine years past"; Robyn Rhysling lost his eye "not five years earlier"; "more than a hundred years" since the last trial of seven; Ser Arlan had not ridden a tilt "many years before"; Ser Humfrey Hardyng died "only last night."
- **chapter_summary:** After burying his master Ser Arlan of Pennytree, the hedge knight Dunk takes the old man's arms and rides to the tourney at Ashford Meadow, taking on a bald orphan stableboy named Egg as his squire. Dunk sells a horse to buy new armor and, with Prince Baelor's intervention, is permitted to enter the lists. When Dunk beats Prince Aerion Brightflame for assaulting the puppeteer Tanselle, he is arrested for striking the blood of the dragon and faces a trial of seven. Six knights and ultimately Prince Baelor Breakspear himself take Dunk's side against Aerion, Daeron, Maekar, three Kingsguard, and the turncoat Ser Steffon Fossoway; Dunk forces Aerion to yield, but Baelor dies of a head wound dealt by his brother Maekar's mace. Egg is revealed to be Prince Aegon Targaryen, and his father Maekar gives him to Dunk as squire, the two riding off toward Dorne.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Spring rains that softened the ground; wet gusty winds; a chill; rain threatening as Dunk dug the grave; first tourney day "bright and clear"; second day "overcast, with a gusty wind blowing from the west"; a soft rain falling "light as dew" the night before the trial; the trial morning rain tapering off at first light leaving fog; "Tendrils of fog were writhing along the ground like pale white snakes"; "pale blowing mist"/"river mist" on the field; "bleak grey sky."
- **Season indicators:** Spring throughout — "spring rains," "spring grass," "the spring air," "at least he had lived to see another spring"; lore that "the summers have been shorter since the last dragon died, and the winters longer and crueler."
- **Time of day:** Sunset (Arlan loved to watch the sunset; Dunk buries him on the western slope); full dark leaving the inn; moonlit, cloudless, star-speckled night riding to the meadow; "It was full dark"; a falling star at night; cock crowing at dawn; torchlit evenings on merchants' row; dawn of the trial.
- **Lighting:** Warm yellow light spilling from the inn windows; torchlight along merchant's row ("a hundred torches"); firelight of campfires; an oil lamp and an iron lantern; a brazier of coals; beeswax candle; "colored rings haloed the scant few pavilions where fires still burned."
- **Sounds:** The old song Arlan sang; a robin heard at the dubbing; a horse whickering; cock crowing; distant tourney music ("pipes and drums"); the ring of hammers/carpenters; trumpets and herald's horns; the crash of lances, ring of steel; the crowd's roar "like the crash of distant waves"; the crack of Tanselle's finger breaking; the screams of Ser Humfrey's dying horse "almost human"; a man screaming "high and terrible"; "the faint thrum of the dragonfly's wings."
- **Smells:** The smell of rain in the air; woodsmoke and bacon; sausages sizzling over a smoky fire; roasting goat basted with honey and herbs.
- **Notable sensory details:** A dragonfly skimming the green pool (recurs at the close); the field churned to "grey-brown mud and torn grass" after rain; the heat and packed mud inside Dunk's greathelm during the trial; the "blue-grey and pulpy" matter and bone visible in Baelor's crushed skull; a green falling star streaking the black sky.

## Characters Present
| Character | Role in Chapter | First Appearance? | Notes |
|-----------|----------------|-------------------|-------|

content.text: I have the full text and format conventions. This is "The Sworn Sword" complete novella (single unit, POV Dunk). Now I'll write the comprehensive extraction.

input.content:
# TSS — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TSS
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Standfast and the Osgrey lands (the Chequy Water, Wat's Wood, the dam) in the Reach
- **location_secondary:** The crossroads with the iron crow cage; the plank bridge over the Chequy Water; Coldmoat Castle (its yard, sept, audience chamber, stables, and maester's tower); the three Osgrey villages; recalled: King's Landing, Dorne, Redgrass Field
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens as Dunk and Egg return from Dosk with wine and find two dead men caged at the crossroads; the stream is found dammed; Bennis cuts a digger; smallfolk are levied and drilled; Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley and learns Ser Eustace was a Blackfyre rebel and the stream is legally the Webbers'; Wat's Wood is burned that night; Dunk quits and then stays; a confrontation at the stream ends in a trial by battle where Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch and nearly drowns; Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne reconcile and wed; Dunk and Egg depart, turning north toward the Wall.
- **time_markers:** Summer; "the drought entering its second year"; Dunk and Egg gone to Dosk "six days"; Ser Arlan "two years dead"; the Redgrass Field / Blackfyre Rebellion "fifteen years ago"; the Great Spring Sickness "two years past" / dead "in the spring"; Egg has served Dunk "a good year and a half"; Aemon measured Dunk in Oldtown "half a year ago"; Rohanne must wed by "the second anniversary of her father's passing" / "the next new moon"; soldiers might "in a fortnight" stand against other peasants; the Field of Fire "two hundred years ago"; the Osgreys "Marshalls of the Northmarch for a thousand years"; Rohanne "five-and-twenty"; her first husband died when she was ten and he twelve; her second husband near "five-and-fifty," she thirteen, the infant son lived "three days"; events span several days ("on the morrow," "evenfall," "come the break of day," night of the fire, the morning of the battle, recovery "the day they took their leave"); rain returns at the close after a long drought.
- **chapter_summary:** The hedge knight Ser Duncan the Tall, sworn sword to the landed knight Ser Eustace Osgrey of Standfast, discovers that the Chequy Water has been dammed by Lady Rohanne Webber, the Red Widow of Coldmoat, after his fellow sellsword Ser Bennis cuts the face of one of her diggers. Sent to parley, Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Widow, learns the stream was granted to House Webber by royal decree because Ser Eustace was a rebel for Daemon Blackfyre, and is slapped for invoking the name of Addam Osgrey, the Widow's dead love. That night Wat's Wood burns; Dunk quits Eustace's service over the old man's lies but returns to make a stand at the stream. When Lady Rohanne demands Bennis or a trial, Dunk cuts his own cheek to pay the blood price and fights her castellan, Ser Lucas the Longinch, in a trial by battle, killing him and nearly drowning himself. Reconciled over Addam's grave, Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne wed, making Eustace lord of Coldmoat; Dunk, healed by the ironborn maester Cerrick, departs with Egg — secretly Prince Aegon Targaryen — riding north toward the Wall.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Blistering summer heat and a years-long drought throughout — "the sky was blue and the sun was blazing hot"; baked roads "hard as brick"; no cloud "anywhere to be seen"; nights "hardly cooler than the days"; a rising wind on the day of battle; dark clouds gathering, then steady rain returning at the close after the drought breaks.
- **Season indicators:** Summer ("the summer sun"); the second year of drought; shriveling melons, dead and dying grain, dry wells, low rivers (Blackwater Rush and the Mander running low), the stream dried to sand and stones.
- **Time of day:** Morning (breaking camp); midday heat; evenfall; moonrise; a sleepless night during the fire; dawn ("come the break of day"); the trial fought under a smoke-darkened daytime sky.
- **Lighting:** Blazing sun; the red glow of the burning wood "engulfing half the western horizon" so it looked as if "the sun was rising in the west"; tallow candles and a fat tallow candle in the cellar; lamplight in the bathing chamber; torchlight in the maester's tower; pale yellow light spilling from Standfast's upper windows.
- **Sounds:** The buzzing of countless flies; crows rising "like a black cloud"; the scrape of whetstone on steel; Bennis's "chicken laugh" and his "bray"; the gurgle of falling water at Coldmoat's moat; hounds barking and singing from the castle sept; the clash of arms; ravens "quorking" in the maester's tower; the steady sound of rain at the end.
- **Smells:** The stench of the two rotting corpses; Bennis smelling "like a bad cheese"; the reek of dead fish in the dry streambed; the carcass of a maggot-ridden tree cat; woodsmoke and ash after the fire; the clove-scented unguent on Dunk's eye-cloth.
- **Notable sensory details:** Two starved dead men crammed face-to-face in an iron crow cage, one gnawed at the other's neck, a tongueless mouth gaping; flies crawling on everything; the green-and-gold of the Chequy Water "but all the gold is gone" when the smoke veils the sun; Wat's Wood reduced to "blackened spears" and "fiery islands in a sea of ash and cinders."

## Events & Actions
1. **Discovery of the caged dead men** — Dunk and Egg find two starved corpses crammed in an iron crow cage at the crossroads; one had gnawed the other, crows had been at both, one is tongueless.
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg
   - Location: The crossroads
   - Outcome: They ride on toward Standfast

2. **Return from Dosk with wine** — Dunk and Egg bring two casks of wine back from Dosk after the krakens raided Little Dosk.
   - Agent: Dunk, Egg
   - Instrument: Maester (the mule, carrying the casks)
   - Outcome: Wine delivered to Standfast

3. **Discovery the stream is dry** — At the plank bridge they find the Chequy Water reduced to sand and stones; Dunk deduces the water "can't have been gone long."
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg, Bennis
   - Location: The plank bridge
   - Outcome: Dunk rides upstream to investigate

4. **Discovery of the dam** — Dunk and Bennis find the Webbers have dammed the Chequy Water and diverted it into Coldmoat's fields by a web of channels.
   - Agent: (built by) Maester Cerrick's design and Coldmoat diggers
   - Location: Half a league south, on the Coldmoat side
   - Outcome: Dunk and Bennis confront the diggers

5. **Bennis cuts the digger Wolmer** — Bennis opens the old digger's cheek "from ear to jaw" with his sword to cow the diggers.
   - Agent: Ser Bennis
   - Patient: Wolmer
   - Instrument: Bennis's longsword
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg, the other diggers
   - Location: The dam
   - Outcome: Wounding; the diggers vow their lady will answer it

6. **Levy and drilling of the smallfolk** — Eustace summons every able-bodied man fifteen to fifty; eight recruits are kept, named for their crops, given fire-hardened spears and woven shields, and drilled by Bennis and Dunk.
   - Agent: Ser Eustace (summons); Bennis and Dunk (train)
   - Patient: The eight recruits
   - Location: Standfast and Wat's Wood
   - Outcome: A poor peasant levy; they break and flee in a mounted charge drill

7. **Eustace tells the tale of the Little Lion** — Ser Eustace recounts how his ancestor Ser Wilbert Osgrey slew King Lancel Lannister with a dagger as he died, saving the Reach.
   - Agent: Ser Eustace
   - Location: Standfast solar
   - Outcome: Establishes Osgrey history and the blood-price idea

8. **Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley** — Sent by Eustace to offer the blood price and ask for the dam's removal, Dunk takes Egg to Coldmoat.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Instigator: Ser Eustace
   - Location: The west way to Coldmoat
   - Outcome: He is admitted to see Lady Rohanne

9. **Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Red Widow** — Ser Lucas lets Dunk address Lady Helicent as the lady of the castle, provoking laughter, until the true Lady Rohanne reveals herself.
   - Agent: Dunk (the error); Ser Lucas (the setup)
   - Patient: Lady Helicent
   - Witness: The Coldmoat court
   - Location: Coldmoat yard
   - Outcome: Dunk is humiliated; Rohanne shows him courtesy

10. **The parley and the royal grant** — Rohanne refuses the blood price and the dam's removal; Maester Cerrick shows the king's sealed grant proving the stream is the Webbers' and Eustace a pardoned rebel.
    - Agent: Lady Rohanne; Maester Cerrick (produces the parchment)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: The royal grant of rights
    - Location: Coldmoat audience chamber
    - Outcome: Dunk learns Eustace fought for the black dragon

11. **Rohanne slaps Dunk** — When Dunk appeals in Addam Osgrey's name, Rohanne strikes him, splitting his lip, and orders him from Coldmoat.
    - Agent: Lady Rohanne
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: Coldmoat audience chamber
    - Outcome: Dunk is expelled; she demands Bennis "by the morrow, else fire and sword"

12. **The burning of Wat's Wood** — That night the wood burns, lighting the western sky so it seems the sun rises in the west.
    - Patient: Wat's Wood (Osgrey land)
    - Witness: Dunk, Egg, Eustace, Bennis, Sam Stoops
    - Location: Wat's Wood, seen from Standfast
    - Outcome: The Osgrey forest destroyed; Rohanne later denies setting it

13. **Dunk quits Ser Eustace's service** — Learning Eustace lied about his sons dying "for the king" (they died for Daemon Blackfyre) and about owning the stream, Dunk returns the cloak and renounces his service.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Ser Eustace
    - Location: Standfast solar
    - Outcome: Eustace casts them out, then they reconcile

14. **Dunk dismisses the smallfolk** — Rather than lead the levy to death, Dunk drives the recruits home at sword point against Bennis's objection.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: The eight recruits
    - Instrument: Dunk's drawn sword
    - Location: Standfast yard
    - Outcome: The recruits flee home; the levy disbands

15. **The stand at the stream** — Dunk, Egg, and Eustace ride to the Chequy Water to face Lady Rohanne's three-and-thirty men; Eustace forbids her to cross.
    - Agent: Dunk (declares he will stop them)
    - Location: The Chequy Water
    - Outcome: Trial by battle is invoked

16. **Dunk cuts his own cheek (the blood price)** — Privately telling Rohanne it was he, not Bennis, who cut the digger, Dunk slashes his own face: "A cheek for a cheek."
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: His dagger
    - Witness: Lady Rohanne
    - Location: Midstream of the Chequy Water
    - Outcome: She is moved but still demands a trial of her innocence over the fire

17. **The trial by battle: Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch** — In single combat in the stream, Thunder's hooves kill the Longinch's courser; the horses fall; Dunk loses his sword, is battered, then drowns Ser Lucas and stabs him under the arm with his dagger.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Ser Lucas Inchfield
    - Instrument: Dunk's dagger; Thunder's hooves (against the courser)
    - Witness: Egg, Lady Rohanne, Eustace, Septon Sefton, the escort
    - Location: The Chequy Water
    - Outcome: Ser Lucas dies; Dunk himself "drowns"

18. **Egg pulls Dunk from the stream; Cerrick revives him** — Egg drags the drowned Dunk out; the ironborn maester Cerrick uses drowned-god techniques to bring him back and treats his many injuries.
    - Agent: Egg (rescue); Maester Cerrick (revival/healing)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Witness: Egg
    - Location: The stream, then Coldmoat's maester's tower
    - Outcome: Dunk survives, broken-boned and scarred

19. **Reconciliation and the wedding** — Rohanne is granted leave to weep at Addam's grave; she and Eustace talk through the night and wed the next morning, Sefton officiating; Eustace becomes lord of Coldmoat.
    - Agent: Septon Sefton (officiates)
    - Patient: Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne (the spouses)
    - Location: Addam's grave; then Coldmoat
    - Outcome: Coldmoat and Standfast reconciled; the chequy lion flies beside the spider

20. **Bennis robs Standfast** — While Dunk made his stand, Bennis bound Sam Stoops and his wife, ransacked Standfast, and fled with everything of value including Eustace's silver cup.
    - Agent: Ser Bennis
    - Patient: Ser Eustace (and Sam Stoops, bound)
    - Location: Standfast
    - Outcome: Bennis escapes; Dunk vows to meet him again

21. **Dunk cuts off Rohanne's braid** — Declining the offered mare and a place as captain of her guard, Dunk kisses Rohanne and cuts off her long red braid to remember her by.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Lady Rohanne
    - Instrument: His dagger
    - Location: Coldmoat stables
    - Outcome: Dunk and Egg ride out on Thunder and the new palfrey

22. **Turning north** — At the crossroads cage, Dunk and Egg choose the road north toward the Wall rather than south to Summerhall.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg
    - Location: The crossroads
    - Outcome: They ride north; "I hear it's tall."

## Spatial Layout & Movement
| Phase | Who | Position / Movement | Relative To | Notes |
|-------|-----|---------------------|-------------|-------|
| Arrival | Dunk, Egg | Ride up to the crossroads cage, then on to the plank bridge | The crossroads / Standfast | Mounted on Thunder; Egg leading Maester |
| Scout | Dunk, Bennis | Ride south and west upstream along the dry streambed to the dam | Standfast / the Chequy Water | Cross to the Coldmoat side at Wat's Wood |
| Confrontation | Dunk, Bennis vs. the diggers | Charge through the grass at the score of diggers | The dam | Bennis cuts Wolmer; three diggers flee |
| Assembly | The eight recruits | Gather at Standfast among the chickens | Standfast yard | Drilled with spears and shields |
| Advance | Dunk, Egg | Ride the west way across the stream into the Widow's power | Coldmoat | "Once we cross the stream, we are in the Widow's power" |
| Departure | Dunk, Egg | Expelled from Coldmoat; ride home through burning night | Coldmoat / Standfast | Wat's Wood afire on the way back |
| Dispersal | The recruits | Driven home in three directions (Melons, Beans, Barleycorns) | Standfast yard | Dunk roars them away at sword point |
| Confrontation | Dunk, Egg, Eustace vs. Rohanne's 33 | Face off across the Chequy Water | The stream | Eustace will not cross; the stream is the battleground |
| Confrontation | Dunk vs. Ser Lucas | Single combat midstream, both unhorsed, fight underwater | The Chequy Water | Dunk kills Lucas, then nearly drowns |
| Departure | Dunk, Egg | Ride out of Coldmoat across the drawbridge into the rain | Coldmoat / the crossroads | End at the crossroads, turning north |

## Information Revealed
| Information | How Revealed | Known To (Characters) | Known To (Reader Only?) |
|-------------|-------------|----------------------|------------------------|
| The Chequy Water and Wat's Wood rights belong to House Webber by royal grant | Maester Cerrick shows the king's sealed parchment; Egg reads it | Rohanne, Cerrick, Eustace, Dunk, Egg | No (revealed to characters in-unit) |
| Ser Eustace was a Blackfyre rebel who chose the black dragon, hoping to regain Coldmoat | Rohanne tells Dunk; Eustace later confirms it | Rohanne, Cerrick, Dunk, Egg, Eustace | No |
| Eustace's sons died fighting for Daemon Blackfyre, not "the king" as he told Dunk | Rohanne; then Eustace ("the rightful king, Daemon Blackfyre") | Rohanne, Dunk, Egg, Eustace | No |
| Eustace's daughter Alysanne was given as a hostage and his wife threw herself from the tower | Rohanne, then Eustace | Rohanne, Dunk, Egg, Eustace | No |
| Egg is Prince Aegon Targaryen, son of Maekar | Narration establishes it; Dunk shows Rohanne the dragon signet | Dunk, Egg; Rohanne infers it from the ring | Partly (reader told outright; Rohanne deduces) |
| The signet hidden in Egg's boot bears Maekar's arms (four three-headed dragons) | Dunk shows it to Rohanne midstream | Dunk, Egg, Rohanne | No |
| It was Dunk, not Bennis, who cut the digger's cheek | Dunk confesses to Rohanne while cutting his own face | Dunk, Rohanne | No |
| Lady Rohanne is twenty-five, not an old woman, and her father's will forces her to remarry by the next new moon | She and Septon Sefton tell Dunk | Coldmoat household, Dunk, Egg | No |
| Bloodraven's true name is Brynden Rivers, a half-Blackwood bastard of Aegon IV, now the Hand | Narration / Septon Sefton | Most characters | No |
| The Great Spring Sickness killed King Daeron II, Valarr, Matarys, and tens of thousands | Septon Sefton recounts King's Landing in the spring | Widely known | No |
| Ser Lucas means to wed Rohanne by force, and her neighbors covet her lands | Rohanne tells Dunk midstream | Rohanne, Dunk | No |
| Bennis robbed Standfast and fled | Maester Cerrick / narration after the battle | Coldmoat, Eustace, Dunk | No |

## Dialogue of Note
| Speaker | Listener | Quote/Paraphrase | Context |
|---------|----------|------------------|---------|
| Narration / Egg | — | "How many eyes does Lord Bloodraven have? A thousand eyes, and one." | The riddle from Oldtown; recurs as Dunk's anxious refrain |
| Smallfolk (reported) | — | "the kinslayer is accursed" | The realm blames Bloodraven and King Aerys for the drought |
| Ser Bennis | Dunk | "Might be this starts with water, but it'll end with blood. Yours and mine, most like." | On discovering the dam |
| Sam Stoops (via Dunk) | — | "She'd sewn poor Dake in a bag o' rocks and sunk him." | How Rohanne drowned the sheep-stealer |
| Egg | Ser Bennis | "I'm not your squire." | Refusing to tend Bennis's horse |
| Lady Rohanne | Dunk | "I trust in moats, ser, and mine will not go dry." | Refusing to take down the dam |
| Ser Eustace | Bennis, Dunk | "the chequy lion still has claws!" | Resolving to fight the Widow |
| Lady Rohanne | Dunk | "Tell Ser Eustace to bring me Bennis… else I will come for him myself with fire and sword." | Expelling Dunk from Coldmoat |
| Ser Eustace | Egg | "Treason… is only a word." | Justifying his choice of the black dragon |
| Ser Eustace | Egg | "Because Daemon was the better man." | Why he chose Daemon over Daeron |
| Dunk (thought) | — | "In his heart the black dragon never died." | Dunk's read of Eustace |
| Lady Rohanne | Dunk | "If you were better born, I'd marry you." | After Dunk cuts his own cheek |
| Dunk | Lady Rohanne | "And if pigs had wings and scales and breathed flame, they'd be as good as dragons." | His answer to her |
| Dunk | Lady Rohanne | "A cheek for a cheek." | Paying the blood price with his own blood |
| Ser Eustace | Lady Rohanne's party | "No bread and salt, not even shade and water… I forbid you to set foot on Osgrey land." | Denying guest right at the stream |
| Maester Cerrick | Dunk | "That's just rain." | The drought breaks as Dunk recovers |
| Egg | Dunk | "Well… I hear it's tall." | Why he wants to see the Wall; the closing line |

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Heat-weary, irritable, and increasingly troubled — sweltering and stubborn; ashamed at his ignorance before Rohanne; wounded and angry to learn Eustace lied; torn between duty and conscience; rueful, smitten, and lonely toward the Red Widow.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Doing right by his oath of service while keeping Egg safe and concealed — and, beneath that, the disputed water, the dam, and the looming fight with Coldmoat.
- **Key decisions made:** To investigate the dry stream; to ride to Coldmoat to parley; to quit Eustace's service over his lies, then to stay and make a stand; to dismiss the doomed smallfolk; to cut his own cheek and fight the trial by battle; to refuse Rohanne's mare and place; to ride north to the Wall.
- **Self-deception flags:** Insists "I'm not like to marry her… I'm Dunk of Flea Bottom"; tells himself he must not think of the Red Widow ("She's not for the likes of you"); clings to "Ser Duncan the Tall" against Bennis's mockery of "Dunk the lunk"; minimizes his own feelings for Tanselle and then Rohanne; reassures himself the dream "never happened that way."

# TSS — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TSS
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Standfast and the Osgrey lands (the Chequy Water, Wat's Wood, the dam) in the Reach
- **location_secondary:** The crossroads with the iron crow cage; the plank bridge over the Chequy Water; Coldmoat Castle (its yard, sept, audience chamber, stables, and maester's tower); the three Osgrey villages; recalled: King's Landing, Dorne, Redgrass Field
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens as Dunk and Egg return from Dosk with wine and find two dead men caged at the crossroads; the stream is found dammed; Bennis cuts a digger; smallfolk are levied and drilled; Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley and learns Ser Eustace was a Blackfyre rebel and the stream is legally the Webbers'; Wat's Wood is burned that night; Dunk quits and then stays; a confrontation at the stream ends in a trial by battle where Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch and nearly drowns; Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne reconcile and wed; Dunk and Egg depart, turning north toward the Wall.
- **time_markers:** Summer; "the drought entering its second year"; Dunk and Egg gone to Dosk "six days"; Ser Arlan "two years dead"; the Redgrass Field / Blackfyre Rebellion "fifteen years ago"; the Great Spring Sickness "two years past" / dead "in the spring"; Egg has served Dunk "a good year and a half"; Aemon measured Dunk in Oldtown "half a year ago"; Rohanne must wed by "the second anniversary of her father's passing" / "the next new moon"; soldiers might "in a fortnight" stand against other peasants; the Field of Fire "two hundred years ago"; the Osgreys "Marshalls of the Northmarch for a thousand years"; Rohanne "five-and-twenty"; her first husband died when she was ten and he twelve; her second husband near "five-and-fifty," she thirteen, the infant son lived "three days"; events span several days ("on the morrow," "evenfall," "come the break of day," night of the fire, the morning of the battle, recovery "the day they took their leave"); rain returns at the close after a long drought.
- **chapter_summary:** The hedge knight Ser Duncan the Tall, sworn sword to the landed knight Ser Eustace Osgrey of Standfast, discovers that the Chequy Water has been dammed by Lady Rohanne Webber, the Red Widow of Coldmoat, after his fellow sellsword Ser Bennis cuts the face of one of her diggers. Sent to parley, Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Widow, learns the stream was granted to House Webber by royal decree because Ser Eustace was a rebel for Daemon Blackfyre, and is slapped for invoking the name of Addam Osgrey, the Widow's dead love. That night Wat's Wood burns; Dunk quits Eustace's service over the old man's lies but returns to make a stand at the stream. When Lady Rohanne demands Bennis or a trial, Dunk cuts his own cheek to pay the blood price and fights her castellan, Ser Lucas the Longinch, in a trial by battle, killing him and nearly drowning himself. Reconciled over Addam's grave, Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne wed, making Eustace lord of Coldmoat; Dunk, healed by the ironborn maester Cerrick, departs with Egg — secretly Prince Aegon Targaryen — riding north toward the Wall.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Blistering summer heat and a years-long drought throughout — "the sky was blue and the sun was blazing hot"; baked roads "hard as brick"; no cloud "anywhere to be seen"; nights "hardly cooler than the days"; a rising wind on the day of battle; dark clouds gathering, then steady rain returning at the close after the drought breaks.
- **Season indicators:** Summer ("the summer sun"); the second year of drought; shriveling melons, dead and dying grain, dry wells, low rivers (Blackwater Rush and the Mander running low), the stream dried to sand and stones.
- **Time of day:** Morning (breaking camp); midday heat; evenfall; moonrise; a sleepless night during the fire; dawn ("come the break of day"); the trial fought under a smoke-darkened daytime sky.
- **Lighting:** Blazing sun; the red glow of the burning wood "engulfing half the western horizon" so it looked as if "the sun was rising in the west"; tallow candles and a fat tallow candle in the cellar; lamplight in the bathing chamber; torchlight in the maester's tower; pale yellow light spilling from Standfast's upper windows.
- **Sounds:** The buzzing of countless flies; crows rising "like a black cloud"; the scrape of whetstone on steel; Bennis's "chicken laugh" and his "bray"; the gurgle of falling water at Coldmoat's moat; hounds barking and singing from the castle sept; the clash of arms; ravens "quorking" in the maester's tower; the steady sound of rain at the end.
- **Smells:** The stench of the two rotting corpses; Bennis smelling "like a bad cheese"; the reek of dead fish in the dry streambed; the carcass of a maggot-ridden tree cat; woodsmoke and ash after the fire; the clove-scented unguent on Dunk's eye-cloth.
- **Notable sensory details:** Two starved dead men crammed face-to-face in an iron crow cage, one gnawed at the other's neck, a tongueless mouth gaping; flies crawling on everything; the green-and-gold of the Chequy Water "but all the gold is gone" when the smoke veils the sun; Wat's Wood reduced to "blackened spears" and "fiery islands in a sea of ash and cinders."

## Events & Actions
1. **Discovery of the caged dead men** — Dunk and Egg find two starved corpses crammed in an iron crow cage at the crossroads; one had gnawed the other, crows had been at both, one is tongueless.
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg
   - Location: The crossroads
   - Outcome: They ride on toward Standfast

2. **Return from Dosk with wine** — Dunk and Egg bring two casks of wine back from Dosk after the krakens raided Little Dosk.
   - Agent: Dunk, Egg
   - Instrument: Maester (the mule, carrying the casks)
   - Outcome: Wine delivered to Standfast

3. **Discovery the stream is dry** — At the plank bridge they find the Chequy Water reduced to sand and stones; Dunk deduces the water "can't have been gone long."
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg, Bennis
   - Location: The plank bridge
   - Outcome: Dunk rides upstream to investigate

4. **Discovery of the dam** — Dunk and Bennis find the Webbers have dammed the Chequy Water and diverted it into Coldmoat's fields by a web of channels.
   - Agent: (built by) Maester Cerrick's design and Coldmoat diggers
   - Location: Half a league south, on the Coldmoat side
   - Outcome: Dunk and Bennis confront the diggers

5. **Bennis cuts the digger Wolmer** — Bennis opens the old digger's cheek "from ear to jaw" with his sword to cow the diggers.
   - Agent: Ser Bennis
   - Patient: Wolmer
   - Instrument: Bennis's longsword
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg, the other diggers
   - Location: The dam
   - Outcome: Wounding; the diggers vow their lady will answer it

6. **Levy and drilling of the smallfolk** — Eustace summons every able-bodied man fifteen to fifty; eight recruits are kept, named for their crops, given fire-hardened spears and woven shields, and drilled by Bennis and Dunk.
   - Agent: Ser Eustace (summons); Bennis and Dunk (train)
   - Patient: The eight recruits
   - Location: Standfast and Wat's Wood
   - Outcome: A poor peasant levy; they break and flee in a mounted charge drill

7. **Eustace tells the tale of the Little Lion** — Ser Eustace recounts how his ancestor Ser Wilbert Osgrey slew King Lancel Lannister with a dagger as he died, saving the Reach.
   - Agent: Ser Eustace
   - Location: Standfast solar
   - Outcome: Establishes Osgrey history and the blood-price idea

8. **Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley** — Sent by Eustace to offer the blood price and ask for the dam's removal, Dunk takes Egg to Coldmoat.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Instigator: Ser Eustace
   - Location: The west way to Coldmoat
   - Outcome: He is admitted to see Lady Rohanne

9. **Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Red Widow** — Ser Lucas lets Dunk address Lady Helicent as the lady of the castle, provoking laughter, until the true Lady Rohanne reveals herself.
   - Agent: Dunk (the error); Ser Lucas (the setup)
   - Patient: Lady Helicent
   - Witness: The Coldmoat court
   - Location: Coldmoat yard
   - Outcome: Dunk is humiliated; Rohanne shows him courtesy

10. **The parley and the royal grant** — Rohanne refuses the blood price and the dam's removal; Maester Cerrick shows the king's sealed grant proving the stream is the Webbers' and Eustace a pardoned rebel.
    - Agent: Lady Rohanne; Maester Cerrick (produces the parchment)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: The royal grant of rights
    - Location: Coldmoat audience chamber
    - Outcome: Dunk learns Eustace fought for the black dragon

11. **Rohanne slaps Dunk** — When Dunk appeals in Addam Osgrey's name, Rohanne strikes him, splitting his lip, and orders him from Coldmoat.
    - Agent: Lady Rohanne
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: Coldmoat audience chamber
    - Outcome: Dunk is expelled; she demands Bennis "by the morrow, else fire and sword"

12. **The burning of Wat's Wood** — That night the wood burns, lighting the western sky so it seems the sun rises in the west.
    - Patient: Wat's Wood (Osgrey land)
    - Witness: Dunk, Egg, Eustace, Bennis, Sam Stoops
    - Location: Wat's Wood, seen from Standfast
    - Outcome: The Osgrey forest destroyed; Rohanne later denies setting it

13. **Dunk quits Ser Eustace's service** — Learning Eustace lied about his sons dying "for the king" (they died for Daemon Blackfyre) and about owning the stream, Dunk returns the cloak and renounces his service.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Ser Eustace
    - Location: Standfast solar
    - Outcome: Eustace casts them out, then they reconcile

14. **Dunk dismisses the smallfolk** — Rather than lead the levy to death, Dunk drives the recruits home at sword point against Bennis's objection.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: The eight recruits
    - Instrument: Dunk's drawn sword
    - Location: Standfast yard
    - Outcome: The recruits flee home; the levy disbands

15. **The stand at the stream** — Dunk, Egg, and Eustace ride to the Chequy Water to face Lady Rohanne's three-and-thirty men; Eustace forbids her to cross.
    - Agent: Dunk (declares he will stop them)
    - Location: The Chequy Water
    - Outcome: Trial by battle is invoked

16. **Dunk cuts his own cheek (the blood price)** — Privately telling Rohanne it was he, not Bennis, who cut the digger, Dunk slashes his own face: "A cheek for a cheek."
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: His dagger
    - Witness: Lady Rohanne
    - Location: Midstream of the Chequy Water
    - Outcome: She is moved but still demands a trial of her innocence over the fire

17. **The trial by battle: Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch** — In single combat in the stream, Thunder's hooves kill the Longinch's courser; the horses fall; Dunk loses his sword, is battered, then drowns Ser Lucas and stabs him under the arm with his dagger.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Ser Lucas Inchfield
    - Instrument: Dunk's dagger; Thunder's hooves (against the courser)
    - Witness: Egg, Lady Rohanne, Eustace, Septon Sefton, the escort
    - Location: The Chequy Water
    - Outcome: Ser Lucas dies; Dunk himself "drowns"

18. **Egg pulls Dunk from the stream; Cerrick revives him** — Egg drags the drowned Dunk out; the ironborn maester Cerrick uses drowned-god techniques to bring him back and treats his many injuries.
    - Agent: Egg (rescue); Maester Cerrick (revival/healing)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Witness: Egg
    - Location: The stream, then Coldmoat's maester's tower
    - Outcome: Dunk survives, broken-boned and scarred

19. **Reconciliation and the wedding** — Rohanne is granted leave to weep at Addam's grave; she and Eustace talk through the night and wed the next morning, Sefton officiating; Eustace becomes lord of Coldmoat.
    - Agent: Septon Sefton (officiates)
    - Patient: Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne (the spouses)
    - Location: Addam's grave; then Coldmoat
    - Outcome: Coldmoat and Standfast reconciled; the chequy lion flies beside the spider

20. **Bennis robs Standfast** — While Dunk made his stand, Bennis bound Sam Stoops and his wife, ransacked Standfast, and fled with everything of value including Eustace's silver cup.
    - Agent: Ser Bennis
    - Patient: Ser Eustace (and Sam Stoops, bound)
    - Location: Standfast
    - Outcome: Bennis escapes; Dunk vows to meet him again

21. **Dunk cuts off Rohanne's braid** — Declining the offered mare and a place as captain of her guard, Dunk kisses Rohanne and cuts off her long red braid to remember her by.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Lady Rohanne
    - Instrument: His dagger
    - Location: Coldmoat stables
    - Outcome: Dunk and Egg ride out on Thunder and the new palfrey

22. **Turning north** — At the crossroads cage, Dunk and Egg choose the road north toward the Wall rather than south to Summerhall.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg
    - Location: The crossroads
    - Outcome: They ride north; "I hear it's tall."

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Heat-weary, irritable, and increasingly troubled — sweltering and stubborn; ashamed at his ignorance before Rohanne; wounded and angry to learn Eustace lied; torn between duty and conscience; rueful, smitten, and lonely toward the Red Widow.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Doing right by his oath of service while keeping Egg safe and concealed — and, beneath that, the disputed water, the dam, and the looming fight with Coldmoat.
- **Key decisions made:** To investigate the dry stream; to ride to Coldmoat to parley; to quit Eustace's service over his lies, then to stay and make a stand; to dismiss the doomed smallfolk; to cut his own cheek and fight the trial by battle; to refuse Rohanne's mare and place; to ride north to the Wall.
- **Self-deception flags:** Insists "I'm not like to marry her… I'm Dunk of Flea Bottom"; tells himself he must not think of the Red Widow ("She's not for the likes of you"); clings to "Ser Duncan the Tall" against Bennis's mockery of "Dunk the lunk"; minimizes his own feelings for Tanselle and then Rohanne; reassures himself the dream "never happened that way."

## Unanswered Questions
| Question | Raised By | Context |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| Who were the two dead men in the crow cage, and what was their crime? | Egg, Dunk | "Who do you think they were, ser?" — never resolved |
| Did Lady Rohanne (or her men) actually set the fire in Wat's Wood? | Ser Eustace, Dunk | She denies it ("I was asleep in my bed"); the cause is never proven |
| Will Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne, both rumored cursed/barren, produce the male heir the grant requires? | (implied by the will and the marriage) | The grant reverts Standfast to the crown if Eustace dies without a male heir of his body |
| Is Dunk himself baseborn or bastard-born? | Dunk | "didn't you ever think that I might be a bastard?… most like you're squiring for a bastard." |
| Will Dunk ever settle his score with Bennis for robbing Standfast? | Dunk | "One day Dunk hoped to meet Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield again" |
| Why did Ser Eustace truly rebel — only for the castle, or for Daemon himself? | Egg | "Why were you a traitor? If it wasn't just the castle." |

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# TSS — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TSS
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Standfast and the Osgrey lands (the Chequy Water, Wat's Wood, the dam) in the Reach
- **location_secondary:** The crossroads with the iron crow cage; the plank bridge over the Chequy Water; Coldmoat Castle (its yard, sept, audience chamber, stables, and maester's tower); the three Osgrey villages; recalled: King's Landing, Dorne, Redgrass Field
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens as Dunk and Egg return from Dosk with wine and find two dead men caged at the crossroads; the stream is found dammed; Bennis cuts a digger; smallfolk are levied and drilled; Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley and learns Ser Eustace was a Blackfyre rebel and the stream is legally the Webbers'; Wat's Wood is burned that night; Dunk quits and then stays; a confrontation at the stream ends in a trial by battle where Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch and nearly drowns; Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne reconcile and wed; Dunk and Egg depart, turning north toward the Wall.
- **time_markers:** Summer; "the drought entering its second year"; Dunk and Egg gone to Dosk "six days"; Ser Arlan "two years dead"; the Redgrass Field / Blackfyre Rebellion "fifteen years ago"; the Great Spring Sickness "two years past" / dead "in the spring"; Egg has served Dunk "a good year and a half"; Aemon measured Dunk in Oldtown "half a year ago"; Rohanne must wed by "the second anniversary of her father's passing" / "the next new moon"; soldiers might "in a fortnight" stand against other peasants; the Field of Fire "two hundred years ago"; the Osgreys "Marshalls of the Northmarch for a thousand years"; Rohanne "five-and-twenty"; her first husband died when she was ten and he twelve; her second husband near "five-and-fifty," she thirteen, the infant son lived "three days"; events span several days ("on the morrow," "evenfall," "come the break of day," night of the fire, the morning of the battle, recovery "the day they took their leave"); rain returns at the close after a long drought.
- **chapter_summary:** The hedge knight Ser Duncan the Tall, sworn sword to the landed knight Ser Eustace Osgrey of Standfast, discovers that the Chequy Water has been dammed by Lady Rohanne Webber, the Red Widow of Coldmoat, after his fellow sellsword Ser Bennis cuts the face of one of her diggers. Sent to parley, Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Widow, learns the stream was granted to House Webber by royal decree because Ser Eustace was a rebel for Daemon Blackfyre, and is slapped for invoking the name of Addam Osgrey, the Widow's dead love. That night Wat's Wood burns; Dunk quits Eustace's service over the old man's lies but returns to make a stand at the stream. When Lady Rohanne demands Bennis or a trial, Dunk cuts his own cheek to pay the blood price and fights her castellan, Ser Lucas the Longinch, in a trial by battle, killing him and nearly drowning himself. Reconciled over Addam's grave, Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne wed, making Eustace lord of Coldmoat; Dunk, healed by the ironborn maester Cerrick, departs with Egg — secretly Prince Aegon Targaryen — riding north toward the Wall.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Blistering summer heat and a years-long drought throughout — "the sky was blue and the sun was blazing hot"; baked roads "hard as brick"; no cloud "anywhere to be seen"; nights "hardly cooler than the days"; a rising wind on the day of battle; dark clouds gathering, then steady rain returning at the close after the drought breaks.
- **Season indicators:** Summer ("the summer sun"); the second year of drought; shriveling melons, dead and dying grain, dry wells, low rivers (Blackwater Rush and the Mander running low), the stream dried to sand and stones.
- **Time of day:** Morning (breaking camp); midday heat; evenfall; moonrise; a sleepless night during the fire; dawn ("come the break of day"); the trial fought under a smoke-darkened daytime sky.
- **Lighting:** Blazing sun; the red glow of the burning wood "engulfing half the western horizon" so it looked as if "the sun was rising in the west"; tallow candles and a fat tallow candle in the cellar; lamplight in the bathing chamber; torchlight in the maester's tower; pale yellow light spilling from Standfast's upper windows.
- **Sounds:** The buzzing of countless flies; crows rising "like a black cloud"; the scrape of whetstone on steel; Bennis's "chicken laugh" and his "bray"; the gurgle of falling water at Coldmoat's moat; hounds barking and singing from the castle sept; the clash of arms; ravens "quorking" in the maester's tower; the steady sound of rain at the end.
- **Smells:** The stench of the two rotting corpses; Bennis smelling "like a bad cheese"; the reek of dead fish in the dry streambed; the carcass of a maggot-ridden tree cat; woodsmoke and ash after the fire; the clove-scented unguent on Dunk's eye-cloth.
- **Notable sensory details:** Two starved dead men crammed face-to-face in an iron crow cage, one gnawed at the other's neck, a tongueless mouth gaping; flies crawling on everything; the green-and-gold of the Chequy Water "but all the gold is gone" when the smoke veils the sun; Wat's Wood reduced to "blackened spears" and "fiery islands in a sea of ash and cinders."

| Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield | Sellsword in Eustace's service | Yes | Mean-mouthed; chews sourleaf; cut the digger; later robs Standfast and flees |

| Coldmoat | Crenellated outer walls thirty feet high; towers at each corner; portcullis and murder hole; a water moat (fallen six feet in the drought) | A modest but true castle; outer ward with kennels, smithy, archery butts, quintain, a seven-sided wooden sept and a stone sept; a chequy lion carved over the main gate beneath the newer spider banners | Audience chamber with sweet rushes and tapestries of tourney and battle scenes; maester's tower lined with herb jars and green-glass vessels, ravens in the rafters | Each corner tower "half again the size of Standfast"; less grand than Storm's End or Highgarden | Well kept; black Webber banners from every turret | Moat fed by the diverted Chequy Water; surrounded by Webber fields and orchards | Gurgle of falling water into the moat; hounds barking; singing from the sept |

## Events & Actions
1. **Discovery of the caged dead men** — Dunk and Egg find two starved corpses crammed in an iron crow cage at the crossroads; one had gnawed the other, crows had been at both, one is tongueless.
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg
   - Location: The crossroads
   - Outcome: They ride on toward Standfast

2. **Return from Dosk with wine** — Dunk and Egg bring two casks of wine back from Dosk after the krakens raided Little Dosk.
   - Agent: Dunk, Egg
   - Instrument: Maester (the mule, carrying the casks)
   - Outcome: Wine delivered to Standfast

3. **Discovery the stream is dry** — At the plank bridge they find the Chequy Water reduced to sand and stones; Dunk deduces the water "can't have been gone long."
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg, Bennis
   - Location: The plank bridge
   - Outcome: Dunk rides upstream to investigate

4. **Discovery of the dam** — Dunk and Bennis find the Webbers have dammed the Chequy Water and diverted it into Coldmoat's fields by a web of channels.
   - Agent: (built by) Maester Cerrick's design and Coldmoat diggers
   - Location: Half a league south, on the Coldmoat side
   - Outcome: Dunk and Bennis confront the diggers

5. **Bennis cuts the digger Wolmer** — Bennis opens the old digger's cheek "from ear to jaw" with his sword to cow the diggers.
   - Agent: Ser Bennis
   - Patient: Wolmer
   - Instrument: Bennis's longsword
   - Witness: Dunk, Egg, the other diggers
   - Location: The dam
   - Outcome: Wounding; the diggers vow their lady will answer it

6. **Levy and drilling of the smallfolk** — Eustace summons every able-bodied man fifteen to fifty; eight recruits are kept, named for their crops, given fire-hardened spears and woven shields, and drilled by Bennis and Dunk.
   - Agent: Ser Eustace (summons); Bennis and Dunk (train)
   - Patient: The eight recruits
   - Location: Standfast and Wat's Wood
   - Outcome: A poor peasant levy; they break and flee in a mounted charge drill

7. **Eustace tells the tale of the Little Lion** — Ser Eustace recounts how his ancestor Ser Wilbert Osgrey slew King Lancel Lannister with a dagger as he died, saving the Reach.
   - Agent: Ser Eustace
   - Location: Standfast solar
   - Outcome: Establishes Osgrey history and the blood-price idea

8. **Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley** — Sent by Eustace to offer the blood price and ask for the dam's removal, Dunk takes Egg to Coldmoat.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Instigator: Ser Eustace
   - Location: The west way to Coldmoat
   - Outcome: He is admitted to see Lady Rohanne

9. **Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Red Widow** — Ser Lucas lets Dunk address Lady Helicent as the lady of the castle, provoking laughter, until the true Lady Rohanne reveals herself.
   - Agent: Dunk (the error); Ser Lucas (the setup)
   - Patient: Lady Helicent
   - Witness: The Coldmoat court
   - Location: Coldmoat yard
   - Outcome: Dunk is humiliated; Rohanne shows him courtesy

10. **The parley and the royal grant** — Rohanne refuses the blood price and the dam's removal; Maester Cerrick shows the king's sealed grant proving the stream is the Webbers' and Eustace a pardoned rebel.
    - Agent: Lady Rohanne; Maester Cerrick (produces the parchment)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: The royal grant of rights
    - Location: Coldmoat audience chamber
    - Outcome: Dunk learns Eustace fought for the black dragon

11. **Rohanne slaps Dunk** — When Dunk appeals in Addam Osgrey's name, Rohanne strikes him, splitting his lip, and orders him from Coldmoat.
    - Agent: Lady Rohanne
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Location: Coldmoat audience chamber
    - Outcome: Dunk is expelled; she demands Bennis "by the morrow, else fire and sword"

12. **The burning of Wat's Wood** — That night the wood burns, lighting the western sky so it seems the sun rises in the west.
    - Patient: Wat's Wood (Osgrey land)
    - Witness: Dunk, Egg, Eustace, Bennis, Sam Stoops
    - Location: Wat's Wood, seen from Standfast
    - Outcome: The Osgrey forest destroyed; Rohanne later denies setting it

13. **Dunk quits Ser Eustace's service** — Learning Eustace lied about his sons dying "for the king" (they died for Daemon Blackfyre) and about owning the stream, Dunk returns the cloak and renounces his service.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Ser Eustace
    - Location: Standfast solar
    - Outcome: Eustace casts them out, then they reconcile

14. **Dunk dismisses the smallfolk** — Rather than lead the levy to death, Dunk drives the recruits home at sword point against Bennis's objection.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: The eight recruits
    - Instrument: Dunk's drawn sword
    - Location: Standfast yard
    - Outcome: The recruits flee home; the levy disbands

15. **The stand at the stream** — Dunk, Egg, and Eustace ride to the Chequy Water to face Lady Rohanne's three-and-thirty men; Eustace forbids her to cross.
    - Agent: Dunk (declares he will stop them)
    - Location: The Chequy Water
    - Outcome: Trial by battle is invoked

16. **Dunk cuts his own cheek (the blood price)** — Privately telling Rohanne it was he, not Bennis, who cut the digger, Dunk slashes his own face: "A cheek for a cheek."
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Instrument: His dagger
    - Witness: Lady Rohanne
    - Location: Midstream of the Chequy Water
    - Outcome: She is moved but still demands a trial of her innocence over the fire

17. **The trial by battle: Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch** — In single combat in the stream, Thunder's hooves kill the Longinch's courser; the horses fall; Dunk loses his sword, is battered, then drowns Ser Lucas and stabs him under the arm with his dagger.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Ser Lucas Inchfield
    - Instrument: Dunk's dagger; Thunder's hooves (against the courser)
    - Witness: Egg, Lady Rohanne, Eustace, Septon Sefton, the escort
    - Location: The Chequy Water
    - Outcome: Ser Lucas dies; Dunk himself "drowns"

18. **Egg pulls Dunk from the stream; Cerrick revives him** — Egg drags the drowned Dunk out; the ironborn maester Cerrick uses drowned-god techniques to bring him back and treats his many injuries.
    - Agent: Egg (rescue); Maester Cerrick (revival/healing)
    - Patient: Dunk
    - Witness: Egg
    - Location: The stream, then Coldmoat's maester's tower
    - Outcome: Dunk survives, broken-boned and scarred

19. **Reconciliation and the wedding** — Rohanne is granted leave to weep at Addam's grave; she and Eustace talk through the night and wed the next morning, Sefton officiating; Eustace becomes lord of Coldmoat.
    - Agent: Septon Sefton (officiates)
    - Patient: Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne (the spouses)
    - Location: Addam's grave; then Coldmoat
    - Outcome: Coldmoat and Standfast reconciled; the chequy lion flies beside the spider

20. **Bennis robs Standfast** — While Dunk made his stand, Bennis bound Sam Stoops and his wife, ransacked Standfast, and fled with everything of value including Eustace's silver cup.
    - Agent: Ser Bennis
    - Patient: Ser Eustace (and Sam Stoops, bound)
    - Location: Standfast
    - Outcome: Bennis escapes; Dunk vows to meet him again

21. **Dunk cuts off Rohanne's braid** — Declining the offered mare and a place as captain of her guard, Dunk kisses Rohanne and cuts off her long red braid to remember her by.
    - Agent: Dunk
    - Patient: Lady Rohanne
    - Instrument: His dagger
    - Location: Coldmoat stables
    - Outcome: Dunk and Egg ride out on Thunder and the new palfrey

22. **Turning north** — At the crossroads cage, Dunk and Egg choose the road north toward the Wall rather than south to Summerhall.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg
    - Location: The crossroads
    - Outcome: They ride north; "I hear it's tall."

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Heat-weary, irritable, and increasingly troubled — sweltering and stubborn; ashamed at his ignorance before Rohanne; wounded and angry to learn Eustace lied; torn between duty and conscience; rueful, smitten, and lonely toward the Red Widow.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Doing right by his oath of service while keeping Egg safe and concealed — and, beneath that, the disputed water, the dam, and the looming fight with Coldmoat.
- **Key decisions made:** To investigate the dry stream; to ride to Coldmoat to parley; to quit Eustace's service over his lies, then to stay and make a stand; to dismiss the doomed smallfolk; to cut his own cheek and fight the trial by battle; to refuse Rohanne's mare and place; to ride north to the Wall.
- **Self-deception flags:** Insists "I'm not like to marry her… I'm Dunk of Flea Bottom"; tells himself he must not think of the Red Widow ("She's not for the likes of you"); clings to "Ser Duncan the Tall" against Bennis's mockery of "Dunk the lunk"; minimizes his own feelings for Tanselle and then Rohanne; reassures himself the dream "never happened that way."

| Did Lady Rohanne (or her men) actually set the fire in Wat's Wood? | Ser Eustace, Dunk | She denies it ("I was asleep in my bed"); the cause is never proven |
| Will Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne, both rumored cursed/barren, produce the male heir the grant requires? | (implied by the will and the marriage) | The grant reverts Standfast to the crown if Eustace dies without a male heir of his body |
| Is Dunk himself baseborn or bastard-born? | Dunk | "didn't you ever think that I might be a bastard?… most like you're squiring for a bastard." |
| Will Dunk ever settle his score with Bennis for robbing Standfast? | Dunk | "One day Dunk hoped to meet Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield again" |
| Why did Ser Eustace truly rebel — only for the castle, or for Daemon himself? | Egg | "Why were you a traitor? If it wasn't just the castle." |

# TSS — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TSS
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk (Ser Duncan the Tall)
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Standfast and the Osgrey lands (the Chequy Water, Wat's Wood, the dam) in the Reach
- **location_secondary:** The crossroads with the iron crow cage; the plank bridge over the Chequy Water; Coldmoat Castle (its yard, sept, audience chamber, stables, and maester's tower); the three Osgrey villages; recalled: King's Landing, Dorne, Redgrass Field
- **approximate_timeline:** Opens as Dunk and Egg return from Dosk with wine and find two dead men caged at the crossroads; the stream is found dammed; Bennis cuts a digger; smallfolk are levied and drilled; Dunk rides to Coldmoat to parley and learns Ser Eustace was a Blackfyre rebel and the stream is legally the Webbers'; Wat's Wood is burned that night; Dunk quits and then stays; a confrontation at the stream ends in a trial by battle where Dunk kills Ser Lucas the Longinch and nearly drowns; Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne reconcile and wed; Dunk and Egg depart, turning north toward the Wall.
- **time_markers:** Summer; "the drought entering its second year"; Dunk and Egg gone to Dosk "six days"; Ser Arlan "two years dead"; the Redgrass Field / Blackfyre Rebellion "fifteen years ago"; the Great Spring Sickness "two years past" / dead "in the spring"; Egg has served Dunk "a good year and a half"; Aemon measured Dunk in Oldtown "half a year ago"; Rohanne must wed by "the second anniversary of her father's passing" / "the next new moon"; soldiers might "in a fortnight" stand against other peasants; the Field of Fire "two hundred years ago"; the Osgreys "Marshalls of the Northmarch for a thousand years"; Rohanne "five-and-twenty"; her first husband died when she was ten and he twelve; her second husband near "five-and-fifty," she thirteen, the infant son lived "three days"; events span several days ("on the morrow," "evenfall," "come the break of day," night of the fire, the morning of the battle, recovery "the day they took their leave"); rain returns at the close after a long drought.
- **chapter_summary:** The hedge knight Ser Duncan the Tall, sworn sword to the landed knight Ser Eustace Osgrey of Standfast, discovers that the Chequy Water has been dammed by Lady Rohanne Webber, the Red Widow of Coldmoat, after his fellow sellsword Ser Bennis cuts the face of one of her diggers. Sent to parley, Dunk mistakes Lady Helicent for the Widow, learns the stream was granted to House Webber by royal decree because Ser Eustace was a rebel for Daemon Blackfyre, and is slapped for invoking the name of Addam Osgrey, the Widow's dead love. That night Wat's Wood burns; Dunk quits Eustace's service over the old man's lies but returns to make a stand at the stream. When Lady Rohanne demands Bennis or a trial, Dunk cuts his own cheek to pay the blood price and fights her castellan, Ser Lucas the Longinch, in a trial by battle, killing him and nearly drowning himself. Reconciled over Addam's grave, Ser Eustace and Lady Rohanne wed, making Eustace lord of Coldmoat; Dunk, healed by the ironborn maester Cerrick, departs with Egg — secretly Prince Aegon Targaryen — riding north toward the Wall.
- **unit_part:** whole

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Blistering summer heat and a years-long drought throughout — "the sky was blue and the sun was blazing hot"; baked roads "hard as brick"; no cloud "anywhere to be seen"; nights "hardly cooler than the days"; a rising wind on the day of battle; dark clouds gathering, then steady rain returning at the close after the drought breaks.
- **Season indicators:** Summer ("the summer sun"); the second year of drought; shriveling melons, dead and dying grain, dry wells, low rivers (Blackwater Rush and the Mander running low), the stream dried to sand and stones.
- **Time of day:** Morning (breaking camp); midday heat; evenfall; moonrise; a sleepless night during the fire; dawn ("come the break of day"); the trial fought under a smoke-darkened daytime sky.
- **Lighting:** Blazing sun; the red glow of the burning wood "engulfing half the western horizon" so it looked as if "the sun was rising in the west"; tallow candles and a fat tallow candle in the cellar; lamplight in the bathing chamber; torchlight in the maester's tower; pale yellow light spilling from Standfast's upper windows.
- **Sounds:** The buzzing of countless flies; crows rising "like a black cloud"; the scrape of whetstone on steel; Bennis's "chicken laugh" and his "bray"; the gurgle of falling water at Coldmoat's moat; hounds barking and singing from the castle sept; the clash of arms; ravens "quorking" in the maester's tower; the steady sound of rain at the end.
- **Smells:** The stench of the two rotting corpses; Bennis smelling "like a bad cheese"; the reek of dead fish in the dry streambed; the carcass of a maggot-ridden tree cat; woodsmoke and ash after the fire; the clove-scented unguent on Dunk's eye-cloth.
- **Notable sensory details:** Two starved dead men crammed face-to-face in an iron crow cage, one gnawed at the other's neck, a tongueless mouth gaping; flies crawling on everything; the green-and-gold of the Chequy Water "but all the gold is gone" when the smoke veils the sun; Wat's Wood reduced to "blackened spears" and "fiery islands in a sea of ash and cinders."

## Location Descriptions
| Location | Defensive Features | Architecture/Layout | Interior Details | Scale | Condition | Surrounding Terrain/Geography | Notable Sensory Details |
|----------|--------------------|--------------------|-----------------|-------|-----------|------------------------------|------------------------|
| Standfast | Steep wooden stair that swings up like a drawbridge; only entrance an oak-and-iron door twenty feet up; lower two stories have only arrow slits | Towerhouse atop a rocky hill; four stories aboveground plus deep vaults and cellars; turrets on two rebuilt corners, ancient grotesques on the other two; flat warped pinewood roof | Hearth and solar hung with rusted weaponry and faded captured banners; bedchamber on the fourth floor, solar below; undercellar well and stone washtub; daub-and-wattle stable at the foot | "A castle only by courtesy"; bigger than it appears; supports three small villages and roughly ten smallfolk | North and west faces pale gray stone above black; partial collapse "a few centuries ago"; roof prone to leaks | Rocky hill seen "for leagues"; crooked single-file path up | Purple moss heaping the stable; hens wandering the yard |
| Coldmoat | Crenellated outer walls thirty feet high; towers at each corner; portcullis and murder hole; a water moat (fallen six feet in the drought) | A modest but true castle; outer ward with kennels, smithy, archery butts, quintain, a seven-sided wooden sept and a stone sept; a chequy lion carved over the main gate beneath the newer spider banners | Audience chamber with sweet rushes and tapestries of tourney and battle scenes; maester's tower lined with herb jars and green-glass vessels, ravens in the rafters | Each corner tower "half again the size of Standfast"; less grand than Storm's End or Highgarden | Well kept; black Webber banners from every turret | Moat fed by the diverted Chequy Water; surrounded by Webber fields and orchards | Gurgle of falling water into the moat; hounds barking; singing from the sept |
| The dam | — | Two stout wooden barricades thrown bank to bank, filled with rocks and packed earth; a ditch cut through Lady Webber's fields, branching into a spider's-web of lesser channels | — | "Not large as such things went, but it looked strong" | Newly built; trees taken from Wat's Wood, bark still on | Spans the Chequy Water on the Coldmoat side, cleared for pasture | Glint of sun on a "score of lesser channels"; black-nosed sheep grazing |
| Wat's Wood | — | Small Osgrey forest; thinning, scraggly, drooping limbs; oaks shedding leaves, pines browning | — | Crossable in an hour or two on horseback | Parched; "one spark, and this will all go up like tinder"; later burned to ash and blackened spears | Bordered by the Chequy Water; once extended to Coldmoat | Maggot-ridden tree-cat carcass; smoke "like a hot gray haze" after the fire |
